instruction: Fix the bug in `case_when_size_common()` where using `NULL` in formula elements (e.g., `case_when(x == "a" ~ NULL)`) throws an internal error 'vec_size_common() should have errored' instead of a meaningful user-facing error message. The function should properly handle the edge case where all conditions or values are NULL, allowing proper error messages to be generated downstream.
context:
The `case_when_size_common()` function in `R/case-when.R` uses `vec_size_common()` to compute sizes of conditions and values. When all elements are NULL, `vec_size_common()` needs the `.absent` parameter to return a sentinel value (-1L) instead of erroring. The fix involves:

1. Adding `.absent = -1L` to both `vec_size_common()` calls
2. Handling the edge case where size_conditions or size_values equals -1L
3. Returning appropriate sizes so that `vec_case_when()` can generate proper error messages

Function signature context:
r
case_when_size_common <- function(
  conditions,
  values,
  size,
  error_call = caller_env()
)

fail_to_pass: case_when(NULL ~ NULL) should error with: '`..1 (left)` must be a logical vector, not `NULL`.'
case_when(TRUE ~ NULL) should error with: '`..1 (right)` must be a vector, not `NULL`.'
case_when(NULL ~ TRUE) should error with: '`..1 (left)` must be a logical vector, not `NULL`.'
case_when(c(TRUE, TRUE) ~ NULL) should error with: '`..1 (right)` must be a vector, not `NULL`.'
case_when(NULL ~ c(TRUE, TRUE)) should error with: '`..1 (left)` must be a logical vector, not `NULL`.'
case_when(TRUE ~ NULL, c(TRUE, TRUE) ~ NULL) should error with: '`..1 (right)` must be a vector, not `NULL`.'
case_when(c(TRUE, TRUE) ~ NULL, c(TRUE, TRUE, TRUE) ~ NULL) should error with: 'Can\'t recycle `..1 (left)` (size 2) to match `..2 (left)` (size 3).'
pass_to_pass: case_when(c(TRUE, FALSE) ~ 1, c(FALSE, TRUE) ~ 2) returns expected values
case_when() with valid inputs continues to work correctly

key_changes: Add `.absent = -1L` parameter to both `vec_size_common()` calls in `case_when_size_common()`
Add conditional logic to handle when size_conditions == -1L or size_values == -1L
Return appropriate size value to allow downstream error handling to generate proper messages
Handle three cases: all values NULL, all conditions NULL, or both all NULL
potential_pitfalls: Forgetting to handle all three edge cases (conditions NULL, values NULL, both NULL)
Returning wrong size value that causes different error messages
Not understanding that -1L is a sentinel value indicating all inputs were NULL
Breaking existing behavior for valid inputs
